reviews.review_msg: Honestly such a great prof. Very passionate about everything he talks about and gave us insightful knowledge about the material. If anything, he made us read 1 novel every 2 weeks, so there is ALOT of reading to get through by the end of the semester. I however was able to survive by skimming and using spark notes. Exams fair, just know the books.

Wouldn't recommend skipping lectures - no notes uploaded, lecture recording, or really anything supplementary sent out. Lectures can seem dull at times, but you gain a better understanding of the books and it will REALLY help your marks. Lots of reading so skimming the books might be more practical.
Chooses interesting course material. Lectures are straight forward but sometimes go off into tangents. Exam is super easy if you attend all the lectures and do all your readings.

Definitely a great first year prof. The content of the class was enjoyable and he was able to make boring lectures bearable. Adds a lot of humour and lame jokes into classes, which is appreciated. Overall, he was a really good prof and I would recommend anyone to take a class with him.
dont play league in class

One of the sweetest, funniest, most interesting profs I've had. His lectures do pretty much consist of him talking about the works, but his thoughts really helped me understand the readings on a deeper level. I had him for the tutorial too and he was nothing but supportive of everyone's ideas. Offers useful feedback too, which I greatly appreciate.
Very interesting and definitely knows his stuff well... Highly recommended :)

Very good professor. He is very passionate and makes lectures very interesting, I'm usually scrambling to write down as much as I can. Is very easy to approach and to bounce ideas off of about poems and passages of novels. Very helpful when you don't understand something. Would definitely take another course with him.
His apocalyptic lit class is one of the best courses I've ever taken at SFU. Love his classic lecture style, would love to do a course like this again.

I was disappointed with this prof after reading such great reviews here. He spent a lot of time reading passages from novels, rather than explaining their significance etc. I felt as though more time should have been spent talking about the novels, rather than reading them to the class. Novels were interesting, nonetheless.

Intelligent man, but personally I found him hard to listen to. Lectures had no structure, no notes or overheads, was complete talk. Has a tendency to ramble and repeat himself which makes it hard to judge what we need to write down and what we can ignore. Some kind of simple overhead/slides with key headings would help in a big way.
Interesting to listen to. Very well read and is clearly passionate about what he teaches. Would definitely take another course with him in the future.
One of SFU's best English profs. A fair marker and just really interesting to listen to. Seems like a really, really nice person and he obviously cares about his students.

great prof overall, even better person. Super knowledgeable but also welcoming and unintimidating while still kind of paternalistic. An admirable man, loves what he teaches. He's the kind of man you'd want for a father in law, I wonder if he has a daughter.
Very enthusiastic and knowledgable about the subject matter.  Made even dry content interesting.  Would definitely take another class with him.

This guy reallys knows his stuff; however, if you don't want to put in the time and do the reading and research, don't take this course. Definitely a great mentor and encourages critical thinking and discussion. Managed to take a very dry subject and make it relateable. Only complaint: has a problem with names (tends to mix up people)
Tom is one of the best prof's at SFU! He made our modernism class entertaining, interesting, and informative. I've grown a new love for Yeats, Pound, and Eliot. That alot, says enough! Tom ROCKS!
This class was hard b/c of its content; it is a study in critical approaches to literature. But he made it manageable, fun, and interesting. Would definately take another class with Tom.
Sooooo Grampa cute!
wonderful mentor, very intellegent and passionate about his subject.  Reminds me of that host on "Daily Planet"
No Comments
if i had to  yeats  my dad, i'd want him to be tom
Tom Grieve rocks! He is the best prof at SFU.
very good professor...knowledgeable, helpful, open to suggestions...good teacher....DEFINATELY TAKE HIM AS PROFESSOR
Likes himself a bit too much.  Lack of substance in teaching-gets by on personality.  Easy course, no real need to go to lecture unless he is also your tutorial leader
BOORing! Save your tuition, although easy marks even if you decide to sleep in.
Such a boring class. He thought he was a lot funnier than he actually was.
Good prof, makes things clear
Awesome upper division prof.  You'll leave his class with your mind still buzzin
Take his Yeats/Pound/Eliot class -- it'll change your life.
very good prof..friendly, funny, knows his stuff
classwasteoftime c/hwkbasedon our opinion/classarehisopinions we can't use his
No Comments
